tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: The core behavioral tests (test_text_contributes_unique_short_text, test_text_contributes_partial_overlap, test_preserved_exclusions) extract the function from source, execute it via Node.js subprocess, and check return values. test_function_exists is text-based but is a gate/static check, not the main behavioral verification. The repo tests actually run npm commands.

test_not_tautological.reason: A stub that always returns True would fail test_preserved_exclusions (which expects False for covered_hello, covered_foo, empty_text). A stub that always returns False would fail test_text_contributes_unique_short_text and test_text_contributes_partial_overlap. A stub returning None fails both. The f2p tests require genuine logic changes.
